paper_id: Goncalves et al. - 2007 - An electrocardiogram (ECG) domain ontology

evidence_quotes.quote: This initiative then  addresses  the  need  for  semantic  interoperability,  in  general,  and  data integration, in particular, in Health Informatics. Besides, it also provides an approach for developing ECG automatic analysis systems.

The ontology  purpose  is  to  provide  a  theory  of  the  ECG  in  order  to:  (i)  address  semantic interoperability  and  data/standard  integration  between  health  systems,  which  is  the currently key point of the research on Health Informatics [Ackerman et al. 2002]; (ii) allow  a  novel  artificial  intelligence  approach  for  ECG  automatic  analysis;  and  (iii) convey a knowledge repository about ECG.
a reference conceptual model that may be used for promoting interoperability in Health Informatics; and (iii) a usefulness formal model for ECG analysis systems.

goals.justification: The authors explicitly frame the ontology's purpose as enabling semantic interoperability, data integration between health systems, and providing an approach for developing ECG automatic analysis systems. This matches the For Intervening signal of enabling interoperability and artifact/system development as the model's intended purpose.

evidence_quotes.quote: The ontology purpose is to bring in a theory of the electrocardiogram (ECG).
The  ontology  purpose  is  to  provide  a  theory  of  the  ECG
The proposed ontology covers the domain from multiple complementary viewpoints. In this way we provide an integral view of the heart activity from the microscopic perspective to the macroscopic one.

goals.justification: The paper repeatedly frames the ontology as providing a theory of the ECG domain and an integral view of heart activity, matching the For Understanding signal of conceptualizing and defining domain concepts.

evidence_quotes.quote: (iii) convey a knowledge repository about ECG.
We advocate that the theory of the ECG conveyed in this paper provides  (i)  a  knowledge  repository  about  this  universe  of  discourse

goals.justification: The authors explicitly state that the ontology serves as a knowledge repository about the ECG domain, which matches the For Documenting signal of knowledge base/repository for asynchronous reference.
